content:
The user wants me to extract the table and text from the provided image. **1. Table Analysis:** The image contains a large table titled "Electrical Characteristics (Tj=25°C unless otherwise noted)". The table is divided into sections: - **STATIC PARAMETERS** - **DYNAMIC PARAMETERS** - **SWITCHING PARAMETERS** I need to transcribe each row and column accurately. **2. Text Analysis:** Below the table, there are several notes labeled A, B, C, D, E, F. - Note A discusses the measurement conditions for $R_{\theta JA}$. - Note B discusses power dissipation $P_D$. - Note C discusses repetitive rating and thermal resistance. - Note D discusses thermal impedance. - Note E discusses static characteristics. - Note F discusses the SOA curve. **3. Footer/Additional Text:** - There is a large block of text at the bottom regarding "APPLICATIONS OR USES AS CRITICAL COMPONENTS...". - There is a note about AOS' products and terms of sale. - The footer contains "Rev 3.1: July 2023", "www.aosmd.com", and "Page 2 of 5". **4. Formatting:** I will use Markdown for the table structure. I will use standard text for the notes and additional information. **Step-by-step transcription plan:** 1. **Header:** Logo and part number. 2. **Title:** Electrical Characteristics... 3. **Table:** - Columns: Symbol, Parameter, Conditions, Min, Typ, Max, Units. - Rows for each parameter (BV_DSS, I_DSS, etc.). - Special handling for merged cells (e.g., Static Drain-Source On-Resistance). 4. **Notes:** Transcribe A-F. 5. **Legal/Disclaimer:** Transcribe the large block of text. 6. **Footer:** Transcribe the footer information. Let's start transcribing the table. **Table Content:** - **Header:** Symbol | Parameter | Conditions | Min | Typ | Max | Units - **STATIC PARAMETERS** - **BV_DSS:** Drain-Source Breakdown Voltage | $I_D=250\mu A, V_{GS}=0V$ | 30 | | | V - **I_DSS:** Zero Gate Voltage Drain Current | $V_{DS}=30V, V_{GS}=0V$ | | | 1 | $\mu A$ - (Sub-row for $T_J=55^{\circ}C$): | | 5 - **I_GSS:** Gate-Body leakage current | $V_{DS}=0V, V_{GS}= \pm 12V$ | | | 100 | nA - **V_GS(th):** Gate Threshold Voltage | $V_{DS}=V_{GS} I_D=250\mu A$ | 0.65 | 1.05 | 1.45 | V - **I_D(ON):** On state drain current | $V_{GS}=4.5V, V_{DS}=5V$ | 30 | | | A - **R_DS(ON):** Static Drain-Source On-Resistance - $V_{GS}=10V, I_D=5.7A$ | | 18 | 26.5 | $m\Omega$ - (Sub-row for $T_J=125^{\circ}C$): | | 28 | 38 - $V_{GS}=4.5V, I_D=5A$ | | 19 | 32 | $m\Omega$ - $V_{GS}=2.5V, I_D=3A$ | | 24 | 48 | $m\Omega$ - **g_FS:** Forward Transconductance | $V_{DS}=5V, I_D=5.7A$ | | 33 | | S - **V_SD:** Diode Forward Voltage | $I_S=1A, V_{GS}=0V$ | | 0.7 | 1 | V - **I_S:** Maximum Body-Diode Continuous Current | | | | 2 | A - **DYNAMIC PARAMETERS** - **C_iss:** Input Capacitance | $V_{GS}=0V, V_{DS}=15V, f=1MHz$ | | 630 | | pF - **C_oss:** Output Capacitance | | | 75 | | pF - **C_rss:** Reverse Transfer Capacitance | | | 50 | | pF - **R_g:** Gate resistance | $V_{GS}=0V, V_{DS}=0V, f=1MHz$ | 1.5 | 3 | 4.5 | $\Omega$ - **SWITCHING PARAMETERS** - **Q_g:** Total Gate Charge | $V_{GS}=4.5V, V_{DS}=15V, I_D=5.7A$ | | 6 | 7 | nC - **Q_gs:** Gate Source Charge | | | 1.3 | | nC - **Q_gd:** Gate Drain Charge | | | 1.8 | | nC - **t_D(on):** Turn-On Delay Time | $V_{GS}=10V, V_{DS}=15V, R_L=2.6\Omega, R_{GEN}=3\Omega$ | | 3 | | ns - **t_r:** Turn-On Rise Time | | | 2.5 | | ns - **t_D(off):** Turn-Off Delay Time | | | 25 | | ns - **t_f:** Turn-Off Fall Time | | | 4 | | ns - **t_rr:** Body Diode Reverse Recovery Time | $I_F=5.7A, dl/dt=100A/\mu s$ | | 8.5 | | ns - **Q_rr:** Body Diode Reverse Recovery Charge | $I_F=5.7A, dl/dt=100A/\mu s$ | | 2.6 | | nC **Notes:** A. The value of $R_{\theta JA}$ is measured with the device mounted on 1in$^2$ FR-4 board with 2oz. Copper, in a still air environment with $T_A = 25^{\circ}C$. The value in any given application depends on the user's specific board design. B. The power dissipation $P_D$ is based on $T_{J(MAX)}=150^{\circ}C$, using $\le 10s$ junction-to-ambient thermal resistance. C. Repetitive rating, pulse width limited by junction temperature $T_{J(MAX)}=150^{\circ}C$. Ratings are based on low frequency and duty cycles to keep initial $T_J=25^{\circ}C$. D. The $R_{\theta JA}$ is the sum of the thermal impedance from junction to lead $R_{\theta JL}$ and lead to ambient. E. The static characteristics in Figures 1 to 6 are obtained using <300$\mu$s pulses, duty cycle 0.5% max. F. These curves are based on the junction-to-ambient thermal impedance which is measured with the device mounted on 1in$^2$ FR-4 board with 2oz. Copper, assuming a maximum junction temperature of $T_{J(MAX)}=150^{\circ}C$. The SOA curve provides a single pulse rating. **Legal Text:** APPLICATIONS OR USES AS CRITICAL COMPONENTS IN LIFE SUPPORT DEVICES OR SYSTEMS ARE NOT AUTHORIZED. AOS DOES NOT ASSUME ANY LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF SUCH APPLICATIONS OR USES OF ITS PRODUCTS. AOS RESERVES THE RIGHT TO MAKE CHANGES TO PRODUCT SPECIFICATIONS WITHOUT NOTICE. IT IS THE RESPONSIBILITY OF THE CUSTOMER TO EVALUATE SUITABILITY OF THE PRODUCT FOR THEIR INTENDED APPLICATION. CUSTOMER SHALL COMPLY WITH APPLICABLE LEGAL REQUIREMENTS, INCLUDING ALL APPLICABLE EXPORT CONTROL RULES, REGULATIONS AND LIMITATIONS. **Footer Text:** AOS' products are provided subject to AOS' terms and conditions of sale which are set forth at: http://www.aosmd.com/terms_and_conditions_of_sale **Footer:** Rev 3.1: July 2023 www.aosmd.com Page 2 of 5 I will now format this into the final response. ```json [{"bbox": [132, 43, 361, 77], "category": "figure", "text": ""}, {"bbox": [790, 45, 883, 60], "category": "header", "text": "AO3400A"}, {"bbox": [134, 109, 540, 124], "category": "title", "text": "Electrical Characteristics (T_{J}=25^{\\circ}C unless otherwise noted)"}, {"bbox": [132, 138, 883, 602], "category": "table", "text": "
